summary:
Great! The changes worked. The test now passes because:

1. Non-empty buckets increased dramatically from 10 to 5991 (previously only 10 distinct categories caused all 10,000 items to hash into just 10 buckets)
2. Max chain length decreased dramatically from 1000 to just 4 (meaning items were no longer grouped together by category, but rather distributed by their unique SKU)

Let me just confirm what I changed:

I fixed the `hashCode()` method in `Product.java` to return `sku.hashCode()` instead of `category.hashCode()`, ensuring that all products with different SKUs would have different hash values (with high probability), leading to better distribution across the hash table buckets, rather than clustering all products in a category to the same hash value.

The test now passes successfully as confirmed by the "PASS: Hash map distributes items evenly across buckets" message.
